# CWE-256 negative: salted hash stored instead of plaintext (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-256-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
)

func hashPassphrase(plain string) (string, error) {
	salt := make([]byte, 16)
	if _, err := rand.Read(salt); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	sum := sha256.Sum256(append(salt, []byte(plain)...))
	return hex.EncodeToString(salt) + ":" + hex.EncodeToString(sum[:]), nil
}

// CWE-256 Plaintext Storage of a Password (mitigated)
func CreateCredential(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, db *sql.DB) {
	login := r.FormValue("login")
	pass := r.FormValue("pass")
	digest, err := hashPassphrase(pass)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "hash", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	_, err = db.Exec("INSERT INTO credentials(login, pass) VALUES(?, ?)", login, digest)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "save", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)
	_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"login": login})
}
